Small Mahler Measures to Degree 200

All known primitive, irreducible, noncyclotomic polynomials with
(multiplicative) Mahler measure M less than 1.3 and degree d at most 200.
Here v is the number of roots outside the unit circle, and only the first
d/2 + 1 coefficients are shown since these polynomials are reciprocal. Only
one of f(x) and f(-x) is listed: if k is the smallest odd integer for which
the coefficient of x^k is nonzero in the polynomial, then the polynomial
where this coefficient is positive is included.

This list is known to be complete through degree 44. In addition, the smallest
Mahler measure for each degree is known to be optimal through degree 54.

A prior list (formerly accessible from the CECM at Simon Fraser University)
showed polynomials through degree 180. The current list includes additional
polynomials reported recently in the literature, as well as 18 new
polynomials (degree 174: 2, 176: 2, 178: 5, 180: 9) that appear not to
have been reported before. In addition, this list extends the prior work by
including polynomials with small Mahler measure up to degree 200.

Two lists of references are included. The first shows papers concerned with
searches for polynomials with small Mahler measure, and sources for the
polynomials in this list. The second is a list of books or survey papers
that treat Lehmer's problem and Mahler's measure. More references can be
found in these publications.

Michael Mossinghoff
m.mossinghoff@ccr-princeton.org
May 31, 2026
